mission.title: Install and Configure MongoDB Community Operator
mission.description: The MongoDB Community Operator allows you to deploy and manage MongoDB Community instances on Kubernetes. It simplifies the management of MongoDB clusters by automating tasks such as scaling and configuration. However, this operator is deprecated, and users are encouraged to migrate to the new MongoDB Kubernetes Operator for continued support and updates.

steps.title: Add the MongoDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the MongoDB Community Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm client.

```bash

helm repo add mongodb https://mongodb.github.io/mongodb-kubernetes-operator

```

> This is the official Helm repo for the MongoDB Community Operator.

steps.title: Install the MongoDB Community Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the MongoDB Community Operator in the 'mongodb' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace mongodb

helm install mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --version 0.13.0

```

> Create a namespace for MongoDB resources.
> Install the operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a MongoDB custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a MongoDB custom resource (CR) to deploy a MongoDB cluster.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.3
EOF

```

> This YAML defines a MongoDB cluster with 3 members.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.3
EOF

steps.title: Verify the MongoDB deployment
steps.description:
Check the status of the MongoDB custom resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb

```

> This command lists the MongoDB resources in the 'mongodb' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the MongoDB Community Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the MongoDB Community Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the MongoDB cluster and all data stored within it.

```bash

helm uninstall mongodb-community-operator --namespace mongodb

kubectl delete namespace mongodb

```

> Uninstall the operator.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the MongoDB Community Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your MongoDB data. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb -o yaml > mongodb-backup.yaml

helm upgrade mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --version 0.13.0

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb

```

> Backup the current MongoDB custom resource.
> Upgrade the operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the MongoDB resources are still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb -o yaml > mongodb-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create MongoDB resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MongoDB custom resource may have invalid specifications.

**Fix:**
Check the custom resource YAML for errors and ensure all required fields are correctly set.

troubleshooting.title: MongoDB pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n mongodb' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: MongoDB service is not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be properly configured or the pods are not running.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the MongoDB pods and services with 'kubectl get pods,svc -n mongodb'.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm repository may not be added or updated.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo update' to refresh the repository and try the install command again.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for MongoDB 5.0, improved CRD validation.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.11, ensure to review the new CRD specifications.

resolution.summary: You should see the MongoDB resource listed with 'kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb' showing the status as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
